commit.message:
Improve project-level resources visibility (#164)

## Summary
- Add resource count to the Resources tab label (`Resources (3)`)
matching the Sessions tab pattern
- Add resource summary row to the project info card with clickable type
badges (e.g., `2 Roles`, `1 Agent`) that switch to the Resources tab
- Add "Install Resource" button next to "Launch Session" in the page
header that opens the install dialog and switches to the Resources tab
- Lift install dialog state from `ProjectResourcesTab` to
`ProjectDetail` so both the header button and the tab's own Install
button share the same dialog

## Test plan
- [ ] Navigate to a project with installed resources — verify tab shows
`Resources (N)` and info card shows type badges
- [ ] Navigate to a project with no resources — verify info card shows
"None installed"
- [ ] Click a resource type badge in the info card — verify it switches
to the Resources tab
- [ ] Click "Install Resource" button in the header — verify it switches
to Resources tab and opens the install dialog
- [ ] Click "Install" button within the Resources tab — verify it opens
the same install dialog

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Adotar metodologia MCGR da Câmara dos Deputados para escala de risco

Substitui a escala de risco genérica (Crítico/Alto/Médio/Baixo) pela
metodologia do Modelo Corporativo de Gestão de Riscos (MCGR) da Câmara
dos Deputados (Ato da Mesa nº 233/2018), com escalas de probabilidade
(1-5) e impacto (1-5), criticidade (P×I) e níveis Muito Alto/Alto/
Moderado/Baixo.

Fix react-grid-layout import for Vite ESM compatibility

Use named imports (Responsive, WidthProvider) instead of default import
with property access, which fails at runtime in Vite's ESM resolution.

Phase 19: rendezvous scanner, NEO data panel, conditional sigma note, option 15

Part A — Immediate fixes:
- A1: _display_detection_result NOTE is now σ-conditional (< 2 INCONCLUSIVE /
  2–5 statistically interesting / ≥ 5 high-confidence candidate)
- A2: new _display_neo_data() panel shows orbital elements, physical props,
  observation arc, completeness, close approaches, sources, nongrav A2
- A3: _detect_single and _detect_multi_evidence call _display_neo_data before
  detection; option 1 now uses verbose=True (researchers always want breakdown)
- A4: _population_pattern_analysis checks for pipeline candidates and offers
  them as a shortcut input source (choice 2 skips file browser)
- A5: _detection_analytics shows ATLAS score tier table for pipeline candidates
  (notable/significant/exceptional); export prompt is '[y/N]' with 'Export skipped.'

Part B — Phase 19A: PA-6 RendezvousDetector Stage 1 (MOID-based, REBOUND-free):
- aneos_core/pattern_analysis/rendezvous.py: PHAMoidScanner + RendezvousPair;
  Drummond D pre-filter, period resonance check, A2 correlation, anomaly scoring
- aneos_core/pattern_analysis/__init__.py: exports PHAMoidScanner, RendezvousPair
- aneos_core/pattern_analysis/session.py: updated rendezvous comment (Stage 1 live)
- aneos_menu_v2.py option 15 _rendezvous_scan: runs PHAMoidScanner, displays table

Part C — Tests: tests/test_phase19.py (15 tests), all pass.
- Updated test_menu_v2_e2e.py to patch _display_neo_data in options 1 & 2 tests
- Test baseline: 277 pass, 0 fail (262 + 15 new)

commit.message:
feat: make landing page fully responsive for mobile and tablet

Replace inline grid styles with CSS classes using media queries at 768px
and 480px breakpoints. Grids stack to single column on mobile, hero mockup
hides, section padding/font sizes reduce, and pricing card scale resets.

Notes:
- Added lp-* CSS classes in globals.css for all landing page grid sections
- Hero: 2-col → 1-col, mockup hidden on mobile, form stays visible
- Stats: 4-col → 2-col on mobile
- SEO vs AEO, How it works, Features, Pricing: stack to 1-col
- Section padding: 80px 32px → 48px 20px (768px) → 40px 16px (480px)
- Section headings: 32px → 24px on mobile
- Nav: padding shrinks, "Masuk" link hidden on mobile, CTA shortened
- Score preview badges hidden on mobile to prevent overflow
- Pricing popular card scale(1.03) removed on mobile
- Hero form gets width:100% for proper mobile fill

restore: Deno + player_client=ios para n-sig do YouTube

Deno é necessário para yt-dlp resolver o n-sig das URLs HLS (301/300).

(sem config file — evita conflitos de PATH/ordem de carregamento).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: utöka SKATTETABELL_31 till 200 000 kr (var 120 000 kr)

Lägger till 8 nya datapunkter (125k–200k). Värdet för 135k är
verifierat mot SKV:s kalkylator (netto 82 298 kr). Värden för
125k och 130k är korrigerade från användarens uppskattning (de var
icke-monotona) via linjär interpolation med ~35,57 % marginalskatt.

Uppdaterar även testets lön från 150k (nu i tabellen) till 250k
för att fortsatt testa extrapolation utanför tabellens övre gräns.

fix: corrigir cálculo e display de suprimento/sangria no PDV

- Corrige bug de precedência: ?? 0 + amount → (?? 0) + amount
- Atualiza estado local após movimento para refletir no header
- Fundo exibe: initial_balance + total_suprimento - total_sangria

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(auth): access control redesign + DB-backed stores [#1059] (#1086)

* feat(auth): access control redesign — entity-centric defineAccess, plans, billing, versioning

Complete access control redesign across 6 phases (Phase 6 deferred):

- Phase 1: Entity-centric defineAccess() with hierarchical role inheritance, 21 validation rules
- Phase 2: Plan features, multi-limit resolution, canBatch(), add-on support, billing periods
- Phase 3: Override store with add/max modes, overage billing computation
- Phase 4: Plan versioning via SHA-256 hashing, grandfathering with grace periods, migration API
- Phase 5: BillingAdapter interface, StripeBillingAdapter, webhook handler, overage computation
- Phase 7: Client-side plan event broadcasting via WebSocket
- DB-backed auth stores: 7 store implementations with dialect-aware DDL

Closes #1072, Closes #1073, Closes #1074, Closes #1075, Closes #1076, Closes #1078

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(auth): update auth guide for DB-backed stores and access redesign [#1059]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): reduce bcrypt rounds in tests to fix MFA test timeouts [#1059]

Use VERTZ_TEST=1 env var (auto-loaded from .env.test by Bun) to reduce
bcrypt rounds from 12 to 4 in test environments. Add explicit timeouts
to slow MFA integration tests. Add global test timeout in bunfig.toml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(server): add type annotations to authModels for isolatedDeclarations [#1059]

bunup treats TS9013 as errors in CI but warnings locally. Adding
explicit ModelDef type annotations to the exported authModels object
satisfies isolatedDeclarations requirements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(server): use Record<string, ModelDef> to avoid isolatedDeclarations cascade [#1059]

Using typeof in ModelDef generics forces non-exported table variables to
also need type annotations (TS9010). Use Record<string, ModelDef> instead
since authModels is internal infrastructure spread into createDb().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix copy trader status polling — read WS state from leaders array

pollCopyTraderStatus() read data.ws_connected and data.auto_mode_enabled
as top-level fields, but /api/copy-trader/status returns them nested
inside data.leaders[]. Fields were always undefined, so the WS status
dot always fell through to "Manual" even when auto-copy was active.

Now finds the current leader from data.leaders[] using currentLeaderId
and reads ws_connected, ws_authenticated, auto_copy_enabled from it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add token budget guard with --compact flag for chat context

Cap each section of the system prompt to prevent blowing past small
model context windows: bandwidth (15/10), known devices (50/25),
network snapshots (200/50), mesh snapshots (200/50) for full/compact
modes. The --compact flag selects tighter limits for smaller models.

ci: add -lws2_32 to Windows FFmpeg link flags for Tesseract

Tesseract's libtesseract.a uses Windows sockets (WSAStartup etc.)
but lists ws2_32 as a PRIVATE CMake dependency, so it doesn't appear
in tesseract.pc's Libs.private. Add -lws2_32 explicitly to the
Windows extra-ldflags to resolve the symbols at static link time.

release: v0.14.4 — force HTTP/1.1 for WebSocket connections (closes #100)

- Force protocols: [:http1] in Mint.HTTP.connect for WebSocket upgrades
  HTTP/2 "extended CONNECT" (RFC 8441) is not supported by most reverse
  proxies (Fly.io, Cloudflare, ALBs), causing extended_connect_disabled
- Extract connect_opts/2 as public function with tests
- Bump all service versions to 0.14.4

responses, and an edge entry point.

- Replace readFileSync with async readFile in image.ts

- Use Buffer.from(png) for Response body compatibility
- Add empty fonts validation in generateOGImage
- Extract shared test-helpers.ts to deduplicate test utilities

Add a minimal JSX factory (h.ts) that produces SatoriElement objects,
enabling templates to use JSX syntax instead of verbose object literals.
No React dependency needed — just a pragma + import per file.

commit.message:
Add `deco chat` — natural language network assistant powered by Ollama

Supports single-query mode (`deco chat "question"`) and interactive REPL
with conversation history. Gathers live router data (clients, network,
mesh, wireless) plus historical DB data (bandwidth, WAN IP history, mesh
uptime, all known devices) and device aliases into a structured system
prompt for the LLM. Degrades gracefully to DB-only mode when router is
unreachable. Streams responses token-by-token with a "Thinking..."
indicator. 21 new tests covering Ollama connectivity, NDJSON streaming,
context gathering (aliases, snapshots, ordering, fallbacks), env var
resolution, cobra wiring, and end-to-end single-shot mode.

commit.message:
Fix sky explorer drag: use consistent camera-frame projection

Replace north-up projectStarsTAN with camera-aware projectStarsCamera
(and vecToPixel) throughout the explorer. The new functions share the
same camera frame math as pixelToVec, making them true inverses.

Drag state now uses (P, R) — a unit vector for view centre and a roll
scalar — instead of (ra, dec, up). Each drag interval applies the
proven rotateByFromTo invariant from the fixed drag-start origin
(P0, R0) rather than accumulating incremental steps, eliminating drift.
Stars now stay locked under the cursor throughout a drag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: web client + ISP proxy, remove iOS extractor-args

player_client=ios via ISP proxy aciona bot-detection do YouTube.
Volta pro web client padrão (sem extractor-args) que retorna
formatos MP4 DASH (137+140/bestvideo+bestaudio) — mesmo approach
do legacy. ISP proxy mantido para passar pelo filtro do YouTube.

Enhance Response Tracker with advanced scoring and filtering

- Add STATE/DEFENSE/ALL toggle to filter questions by side
- Sort STATE questions before DEFENSE in display
- Add column headers with side labels (Q1-S, Q2-D, etc)
- Implement struck juror filtering from localStorage
- Change score calculation from weighted normalized to simple sum of numerical values
- Add Top 15/Bottom 15 juror ranking with row color coding (green/red/amber)
- Add Recalc Scores button to refresh calculations
- Remove Progress column for cleaner interface
- Change YES_NO display from "Yes"/"No" to compact "Y"/"N"
- Display Y/N values in table cells alongside checkmarks

Features:
- Scores now sum SCALED values and YES_NO as 1/0, skip OPEN_ENDED
- Green row tint for top 15 favorable jurors
- Red row tint for bottom 15 unfavorable jurors
- Amber text for middle performers
- useCallback for loadData to support recalculation button

commit.message:
Refs #743 — optimize move --recursive to use sub-issue API

Replace full GetProjectItems scan with targeted
GetProjectItemsByIssues queries for recursive moves. Sub-issue tree
is discovered via GetSubIssuesBatch, then project items for
discovered sub-issues are fetched with a second targeted query.
This avoids scanning the entire project for recursive operations.
